checks.finding: Every inline decision carries a stated rationale: Design Decisions section resolves OQ-001..OQ-004 each tied to a REQ/ADR reason (e.g. OQ-003 'building such a mechanism is explicitly out of scope... remains an open automation gap'); the two new decisions (design.md lines 554-567) state explicit 'Decided:' rationale ('a bare none-vs-absent string sentinel would be more fragile... than an explicit, typed second flag'; 'following this repository's established append-only ADR convention'). Technology choices (Bash+PowerShell twins, python3 heredoc, JSON contract) carry rationale in frontend-spec.md's Technology Stack table (Rationale column, e.g. 'cross-host determinism, matches select-agent-model.sh's existing implementation technique'). No unjustified 'we will use X' statement found.

design.md's '## Open Questions' section (lines 700-707) contains no unresolved entries: 'None blocking. All investigation.md OQ-001..OQ-004 are resolved above with design decisions.' Each of OQ-001..OQ-004 is independently resolved in the Design Decisions section with an owning rationale, and requirements.md's own Open Questions section (lines 941-965) marks OQ-001/002/004 RESOLVED and OQ-003 PARTIALLY RESOLVED with an explicit non-blocking Non-goals disposition (documented procedure, automation gap explicitly deferred). No entry lacks an owner or resolution path because no blocking entry exists.

investigation.md is present (INV-001..INV-014, OQ-001..OQ-004), so the absent-investigation Major rule does not apply. Each of design.md's '## Assumptions' entries (lines 679-698) is grounded: select-agent-model.sh's eligibility/escalation logic stability cites exact lines (select-agent-model.sh:232-247, :155-184); the Codex .toml comment-free state is 'verified directly'; A3 ancestor status is grounded in a stated git merge-base check matching investigation.md's own Assumptions (lines 904-912); the round-2 PROTECTED_GATE_SUFFIXES re-verification (lines 694-698) cites guard_invariants.py:4 directly. All four qualify as either investigation-grounded or directly file:line-verified technical facts.

design.md has a '## Constraint Compliance' section (lines 656-677, Primary/TYPE-D path used). It addresses every constraint identifiable in requirements.md: v1-frozen byte-identity (REQ-001/AC-004), welded golden baseline (REQ-002/005), xhigh gate preservation, malformed-registry fail-closed rejection (AC-054), the four-plus-one protected-file write boundary (REQ-003/005, round-2 test.yml correction), twin-pair mandate, host-independent cross-host degradation (REQ-004/006/008), run-record truthfulness (REQ-004/006), CLI-injection resistance (REQ-006/AC-052), T-007's release-ordering gate (REQ-007), doc-following/version-bump discipline (REQ-009), and the three CI-resilience edge cases (bash 3.2 set -u, macOS $TMPDIR, Windows jq CRLF) from requirements.md's Edge Cases section. No requirements.md constraint was found unaddressed.

No performance requirements found in requirements.md. Grep across requirements.md/acceptance-tests.md/infra-spec.md for latency/throughput/response-time/SLA/ms/concurrency keywords returned no numeric threshold or SLA statement; infra-spec.md's Runtime Budget section explicitly states 'no new LOOP_SUITE_BUDGET_SECONDS-style runtime-budget constant is introduced,' confirming this feature carries no performance SLA.

design.md's '## Deployment / CI Plan' section (lines 628-654) states the deployment target ('No runtime deployment'), the feature-flag-equivalent rollout mechanism ('--effort-policy welded -> matrix' default flip, hardcoded in select-agent-model, changed by exactly one code edit in T-007, welded remains explicitly available indefinitely), and concrete CI pipeline changes (three new suite pairs joining tests/run-all.sh/.ps1 directly plus human-copy-staged .github/workflows/test.yml registration, render-agent-frontmatter --check joining tests/validate-repository.ps1). No database migration execution order applies because Data Plan (lines 265-269) states 'Migration Strategy: none... v1/v2 coexist, no in-place migration,' so the migrate-before/after-deploy requirement is not triggered.

design.md's '## Data Plan' Migration Strategy subsection (lines 265-269) states: 'none for the registry (v1/v2 coexist, no in-place migration). Run-record v2 is additive-only; no v1 record is rewritten or migrated -- emit-run-record simply emits v1 shape when no --effort-* flag is supplied and v2 shape when one is, and the validator accepts both shapes keyed off the schema field.' This is functionally equivalent to a 'No data changes' declaration with explicit rationale (additive-only schema evolution, no destructive rewrite, no >10k-row table involved), satisfying the check's no-migration-needed condition; rollback is additionally covered in Deployment/CI Plan's Rollback paragraph (each of T-001..T-006 independently revertible, additive by construction).

design.md's '## External Integrations' section (lines 621-626) names the sole external system this feature touches -- the pre-existing 'codex CLI' invocation at run-panelist-gpt.sh:146 -- states it is extended with one additional flag and that 'no new network call, service, or third-party action is introduced.' The integration's contract (exact CLI flags --model/--effort/--no-project-doc) is detailed in API/Contract Plan's T-006 subsection (lines 463-475), and its failure behaviour is documented in infra-spec.md's Runtime Dependencies table ('codex CLI... graceful degrade already established at run-panelist-gpt.sh:62-69 (absent CLI -> exit 1, non-zero, not a tool error)'), which design.md's Layer Specifications table (line 175) points to as the canonical Infrastructure detail. No new, undocumented integration was found referenced elsewhere in design.md.

design.md's Components table and API/Contract Plan map 1:1 onto requirements.md's REQ-001..REQ-009 (T-001..T-007): v2 registry (REQ-001), selector flags (REQ-002), render-agent-frontmatter (REQ-003), run-record v2 (REQ-004), routing test expansion + test.yml registration (REQ-005), Codex effort application (REQ-006), Phase-2 flip (REQ-007), cross-host degradation (REQ-008, Constraint Compliance row), and doc/version-bump discipline (REQ-009, Global Constraints CHANGELOG fan-out). No component, data entity, or CLI flag was found in design.md without a traceable REQ/AC in the Cross-Layer Dependencies table (lines 185-197); no Goal or User Story in requirements.md was found uncovered.

High-risk surfaces are present (protected-file write-boundary/authorization change spanning five R-10 protected files; CLI-argument-injection prevention for the codex invocation; run-record truthfulness/repudiation risk) and each names a concrete acceptance-test artifact: TEST-019/TEST-020 (write-boundary positive/read-boundary proof), TEST-027 (three-part staged/live/applied test.yml registration proof), TEST-035..038/TEST-052 (argv-composition and injection-rejection proof), TEST-023/TEST-051 (run-record truthfulness field-population proof) -- all cited by ID in design.md's Constraint Compliance table and cross-referenced in security-spec.md's Security Tests table. No applicable high-risk surface was found without a named verification path.
